title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given.

questions.firstSentence: I haven't seen such a good film for ages.
questions.correct: UNLIKELY (that) Jo | will/’ll leave / move (away) from / out of
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'a long time since I've seen', using the present perfect after 'since'.

questions.firstSentence: The last time I went to the cinema was months ago.
questions.explanation: Use present perfect with 'for months': 'haven't been'.

questions.firstSentence: They say that the company is losing money.
questions.explanation: Passive reporting structure: 'is said to be'.

questions.firstSentence: He regrets not studying harder at school.
questions.explanation: Third conditional wish: 'wishes he had studied'.

questions.firstSentence: It isn't necessary for you to come early tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'Don't need to come' expresses lack of necessity.

questions.firstSentence: She started learning French three years ago.
